title: Coil array
abstract: A coil array includes a first bar (701, 801) comprising a first gap (820) and a first winding (703, 821) around the first bar, a second bar (711, 803) comprising a second gap and a second winding (713) around the second bar, a lid element (720) connected to an end of each of the first and second bars, and a floor element (730) connected to another end of each of the first and second bars, the first bar, the second bar, the lid element, and the floor element being formed of a ferromagnetic material, and the first gap and the second gap being formed of air or a material having a magnetic permeability lower than the magnetic permeability of the ferromagnetic material.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Coil array comprising: a first bar (701 , 801 ) comprising a first gap (820) and a first winding (703, 821 ) around the first bar; a second bar (71 1 , 803) comprising a second gap and a second winding (713) around the second bar; a lid element (720) connected to an end of each of the first and second bars; and a floor element (730) connected to another end of each of the first and second bars, the first bar, the second bar, the lid element, and the floor element being formed of a ferromagnetic material, and the first gap and the second gap being formed of air or a material having a magnetic permeability lower than the magnetic permeability of the ferromagnetic material. Coil array as claimed in claim 1 , comprising: a loopback bar (740) made of a ferromagnetic material and continuously formed between the lid element and the floor element and being arranged between the first bar (701 ) and the second bar (71 1 ), the first winding (703) and the second winding (713) being configured such that a direction of first magnetic field lines inside the first bar is identical to a direction of second magnetic field lines inside the second bar. Coil array as claimed in claim 2, wherein a second termination of the first winding and a second termination of the second winding are connected, 

 a first termination of the first winding being separate from a first termination of the second winding. Coil array as claimed in claim 2 or 3, further comprising a third bar (721 ) comprising a third gap and a third winding, wherein the loopback bar (740) is arranged between the first, the second, and the third bars. Coil array as claimed in any of claims 1 to 3, which comprises k bars having an air gap each, wherein k > 3, a loopback bar (740) further being arranged between the k bars, so that an axis of the loopback bar forms a center of a circle on which axes of the k bars are arranged, angles between two adjacent axes, respectively, being identical within a tolerance range, the tolerance range being Â± 10% of 360Â° divided by k. Coil array as claimed in any of the previous claims, wherein all k windings are wound such that magnetic field lines inside all k bars are identical. Coil array as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising: a third bar (802) comprising a third gap and a third winding around the third bar; a fourth bar (804) comprising a fourth gap and a fourth winding around the fourth bar, the second bar (803) being arranged between the first bar (801 ) and the third bar (802), the third bar (802) being arranged between the second bar (803) and the fourth bar (804), and the fourth bar (804) being arranged between the third bar (802) and the first bar (801 ), 

 the first winding being connected in series with the third winding so as to obtain a magnetic field direction present inside the first winding that is opposite to the magnetic field direction present inside the third winding, the second winding being connected in series with the fourth winding so as to obtain a magnetic field direction present inside the second winding that is opposite to a magnetic field direction present inside the third winding. Coil array as claimed in claim 7, wherein a series connection comprised of the first and third windings (81 1 , 812) is connected in parallel with a series connection comprised of the second winding and the fourth winding. Coil array as claimed in claim 1 or claim 7, comprising I bars comprising an air gap each, I being even-numbered andâ¥ 4, wherein axes of the i bars are arranged on a circle, angles between two adjacent axes, respectively, being identical within a tolerance range, the tolerance range being Â± 10% of 360Â° divided by I. Coil array as claimed in any of claims 7 to 9, further comprising one or several fastening elements (850) arranged between the floor element (730) and the lid element (720) so as to hold the coil array together. Coil array as claimed in claim 10, wherein the one or the several fastening elements have a magnetic permeability lower than a magnetic permeability of the ferromagnetic material. Coil array as claimed in any of claims 1 , 7 to 1 1 , wherein each bar is configured of a first bar section (720), a second bar section mounted on the floor element (730), and an inlay disk (820) made of a non- ferromagnetic material for forming the gap. Coil array as claimed in any of the previous claims, wherein the first winding, the second winding, or one of several further windings each extend across an air gap inside the bar on which the winding is arranged. 14. Switching power supply comprising: a plurality of parallel branches, each parallel branch comprising two serially connected controllable switches; and a coil array as claimed in any of the previous claims, a first termination of the first winding being connected to a point at which the first and second switches (1 10, 1 12), are connected inside a parallel branch, and a second termination of the second winding being connected, in a conducting manner, to a point at which the first and second switches (120, 122) of a further parallel branch are connected to each other. 15. Switching power supply as claimed in claim 14, wherein the coil array is configured as claimed in any of claims 7 to 12, and wherein a second termination of the first winding is connected to a first termination of the third winding, wherein a second termination of the second winding is connected to a first termination of the fourth winding, wherein a second termination of the third winding and a second termination of the fourth winding are both connected to a first terminal of a capacitor (152), and wherein a second terminal of the capacitor is connected to a circuit ground (422).
